hypothesis: Incorporating explicit many-body dispersion (MBD) corrections into uMLIP training data for MOFs—rather than the pairwise DFT-D3(BJ) scheme used as the DFT reference throughout MOFSimBench—will yield a disproportionately larger improvement in bulk modulus prediction accuracy compared to heat capacity prediction accuracy, because the collective long-range polarization interactions captured by MBD predominantly govern the mechanical stiffness of the porous framework rather than its vibrational density of states at 300 K.
